text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY REPORT
BONE MARROW BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE -- POST-INDUCTION RESPONSE ASSESSMENT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen ID: BM-2024-RVO-002
Patient: Vasquez-Okonkwo, Renata
Date of Procedure: Day +35 post-initiation of induction chemotherapy
Submitting Clinician: Hematology-Oncology / Leukemia Service
Specimen Type: Bilateral posterior iliac crest bone marrow core biopsy (right side, 2.1 cm; left side, 1.8 cm) and aspirate, plus touch imprint preparations
Clinical Indication: Response assessment following induction chemotherapy for acute myeloid leukemia (AML, NOS, FAB M1, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, trisomy 8). Patient received cytarabine 100 mg/m2/day x 7 days + daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 days 1-3 + midostaurin 50 mg BID days 8-21.
Prior Relevant Pathology: BM-2024-RVO-001 (diagnostic bone marrow biopsy): Hypercellular marrow, 95% cellularity, 74% myeloblasts by aspirate differential. AML, NOS, myeloblastic without maturation. Blasts positive for CD34, CD117, CD13, CD33, CD123, HLA-DR, MPO; negative for CD19, CD3, CD56, TdT. Cytogenetics: 46,XX,+8[12]/46,XX[8]. FLT3-ITD allelic ratio 0.91 (HIGH), DNMT3A R882H, TET2 Q1299*, WT1 R462W, NRAS Q61H, KDM6A S1025fs, STAG2 E253*, BCOR G1522fs.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS PATHOLOGY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Received are two formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded core biopsy cylinders from bilateral posterior iliac crests and a Wright-Giemsa stained aspirate smear preparation along with clot sections. The right core biopsy measures 2.1 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter; the left core measures 1.8 cm x 0.2 cm. Both cores are grossly pale tan-white and firm without grossly identifiable areas of hemorrhage or necrosis. The clot section is a 0.4 x 0.3 cm tan aggregate. Five aspirate smears and four touch preparations were submitted for morphologic and flow cytometric evaluation. Additional aspirate material was submitted in RPMI for flow cytometry MRD panel, and separate aliquots were submitted for molecular studies (FLT3-ITD PCR, WT1 quantitative PCR). Decalcified sections from both cores prepared; hematoxylin & eosin (H&E), Giemsa, and reticulin stains performed.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS -- MORPHOLOGY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Bone Marrow Biopsy (H&E and Giemsa stains, bilateral cores):
The biopsy cores are markedly HYPOCELLULAR. Estimated overall marrow cellularity is 10-15% (reference range for age: approximately 50-60%). The adipocytes are prominent and constitute the majority of the marrow space. The residual hematopoietic elements are distributed in small, loosely clustered foci scattered throughout the adipose tissue. No sheets, clusters, or aggregates of blasts are identified. There is no evidence of residual leukemic infiltrate on core biopsy.

The residual hematopoietic islands, while small in number, demonstrate evidence of trilineage hematopoiesis:
- Erythropoiesis: Small erythroid islands are present. Erythroid precursors appear morphologically normal without dysplastic features. Normoblastic maturation is appreciated.
- Granulopoiesis: Scattered myeloid precursors are seen with apparent left-shift consistent with early marrow regeneration post-chemotherapy. No Auer rods identified. No excess blasts morphologically.
- Megakaryopoiesis: Occasional megakaryocytes are identified, including forms with normal polylobated nuclei. No megakaryocyte clustering or dysplastic forms (hypogranulation, hypolobation) are identified beyond what is expected in a regenerating post-chemotherapy marrow.

Blast Estimation (morphology): Myeloblasts estimated at less than 3% of nucleated marrow cells by morphologic assessment on H&E and touch imprint preparations. No Auer rods identified in the current specimen.

Reticulin Stain: Grade 0-1 fibrosis (focal fine reticulin fibers without collagen deposition). No significant increase in reticulin compared to the diagnostic specimen.

No granulomas, no lymphoid aggregates, no plasma cell excess, no evidence of myeloid sarcoma or extramedullary extension.

Impression (Morphology): Markedly hypocellular marrow consistent with post-induction aplasia/hypoplasia with early trilineage regeneration. No morphologic evidence of residual acute myeloid leukemia.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS -- BONE MARROW ASPIRATE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Aspirate Smears (Wright-Giemsa):
The aspirate smears are of adequate cellularity for evaluation. A 200-cell differential count was performed.

Aspirate Differential (200 cells counted):
- Myeloblasts: 2.5%
- Promyelocytes: 4.0%
- Myelocytes: 8.5%
- Metamyelocytes: 9.0%
- Band forms: 14.5%
- Segmented neutrophils: 21.0%
- Eosinophils/precursors: 3.5%
- Basophils: 0.5%
- Proerythroblasts: 2.0%
- Basophilic normoblasts: 6.5%
- Polychromatophilic normoblasts: 11.5%
- Orthochromatic normoblasts: 8.0%
- Lymphocytes: 6.5%
- Monocytes: 2.0%
- Plasma cells: 0.5%
- Megakaryocytes: seen (not counted in differential)
Total: 100%

Myeloblast fraction: 2.5% (below the 5% threshold defining AML; below the 5% threshold for MDS with excess blasts; consistent with complete morphologic remission).

Morphologic assessment of blasts: The rare cells classified as myeloblasts are not appreciably atypical. No Auer rods are identified. No aberrant granulation pattern is appreciated. These appear to represent normal regenerating blast forms rather than residual leukemic blasts.

Dysmorphic features: Mild erythroid dyspoiesis noted in a minority of normoblasts (occasional binucleate forms, estimated <5%), interpreted as residual chemotherapy effect and not diagnostic of myelodysplasia in the post-treatment context. Granulopoiesis without significant dysplastic features. Megakaryocytes with normal morphology.

M:E ratio: Approximately 2.4:1 (mildly elevated, consistent with erythroid suppression in recovery phase).

Clot Section: Confirms hypocellular marrow with small hematopoietic foci and no excess blasts. No fibrin thrombi.

Impression (Aspirate): Bone marrow aspirate with myeloblasts comprising 2.5% of nucleated marrow elements. Morphologic complete remission (CR) by WHO and ELN 2022 criteria (<5% blasts; no Auer rods; no residual blast clusters). Trilineage hematopoiesis with features of early marrow regeneration post-intensive chemotherapy.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. FLOW CYTOMETRY -- MULTI-PARAMETER MINIMAL RESIDUAL DISEASE (MRD) PANEL
   Method: 10-color multi-parameter flow cytometry (MFC), bone marrow aspirate
   Total events acquired: 612,400 CD45-positive events
   Sensitivity: 0.01% (1 in 10,000 marrow cells)
   Gating Strategy: CD45/SSC gating with hierarchical antibody panels targeting the previously defined aberrant blast immunophenotype (CD34+/CD117+/CD13+/CD33+/CD123+/HLA-DR+/MPO+ with CD56-negative, CD19-negative, CD3-negative, TdT-negative)

   Result: NEGATIVE -- NO ABERRANT BLAST IMMUNOPHENOTYPE DETECTED.

   The blast gate (CD34+/CD117+ events within the CD45-dim/SSC-low region) accounted for less than 0.01% of total CD45+ events. No population with the diagnostic aberrant immunophenotype (CD34+CD117+CD123+CD33+CD13+HLA-DR+ with lineage negativity) was identified at the sensitivity of this assay. Normal CD34+ progenitors are present at expected low levels for a post-chemotherapy regenerating marrow, without aberrant co-expression patterns.

   Normal hematopoietic populations identified: maturing granulocytes with normal antigen expression pattern, monocytes with normal CD14/CD64/HLA-DR expression, erythroid precursors, B-cell precursors (hematogones, Stage I-III) in low numbers consistent with early regeneration, T and NK cells in expected proportions.

   Interpretation: MRD-negative by multi-parameter flow cytometry. This corresponds to a greater than 4-log reduction from the diagnostic blast burden.

2. MOLECULAR MRD -- FLT3-ITD POLYMERASE CHAIN REACTION
   Method: Capillary electrophoresis-based fragment analysis PCR for FLT3-ITD detection (33-base pair tandem duplication, exon 14)
   Specimen: Bone marrow aspirate
   Sensitivity: 10^-4 (0.01%) based on internal controls and validated assay performance
   Reference: At diagnosis, FLT3-ITD VAF 48.2%, allelic ratio 0.91.

   Result: FLT3-ITD NOT DETECTED (below limit of detection).

   No FLT3-ITD fragment of the diagnostic size (33-bp insertion) was detected on capillary electrophoresis. Wild-type FLT3 band present and adequate. Assay controls valid.

   Interpretation: FLT3-ITD MRD-negative by PCR. Given the high diagnostic VAF (48.2%), this represents a reduction of at least 4 logs from baseline.

3. WT1 QUANTITATIVE PCR
   Method: Quantitative RT-PCR for WT1 transcript expression normalized to ABL1 housekeeping gene
   Specimen: Bone marrow aspirate
   Institutional Normal Range: less than 50 copies per 10^4 ABL1 copies in bone marrow

   Result: WT1 transcript level = 26 copies per 10^4 ABL1 copies.

   Interpretation: WITHIN NORMAL LIMITS. WT1 level is within the normal reference range for bone marrow. No elevation above the institutional threshold for WT1-MRD positivity. (Note: at diagnosis, WT1 was significantly elevated [exact diagnostic quantification not reported in current assay system]; current level in normal range is consistent with deep remission.)

4. CYTOGENETICS
   Conventional cytogenetics (G-banding) was NOT repeated on this specimen per institutional protocol for post-induction day 35 assessment (karyotype to be reassessed at later time points if clinically indicated). Molecular cytogenetics deferred.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PERIPHERAL BLOOD -- CONCURRENT CBC (Day +35)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The following peripheral blood counts were obtained concurrently with bone marrow biopsy:
- WBC: 3,200/uL
- Absolute Neutrophil Count (ANC): 1,850/uL
- Hemoglobin: 9.8 g/dL
- MCV: 94 fL
- Platelets: 112,000/uL
- Absolute lymphocyte count: 980/uL
- Absolute monocyte count: 240/uL
- Circulating blasts: NOT DETECTED on manual differential (200-cell manual differential reviewed)

Peripheral blood morphology: No circulating blasts. Mild normochromic normocytic anemia. Recovering leukopenia, platelet count recovering. Occasional hypersegmented neutrophils consistent with post-chemotherapy effect. No Pelger-Huet anomaly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (IHC)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CD34 immunostain (bone marrow core biopsy): Rare scattered CD34-positive progenitor cells, fewer than 1% of marrow cells. No clusters or aggregates of CD34-positive cells. Normal distribution pattern.
CD117 (c-Kit): Rare scattered positive mast cells and progenitors. No abnormal clusters.
MPO: Positive in granulocytic lineage cells in a normal maturation pattern.
CD3 and CD20: Occasional scattered T and B lymphocytes without excess.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

BONE MARROW BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE, BILATERAL POSTERIOR ILIAC CREST (DAY +35 POST-INDUCTION):

COMPLETE MORPHOLOGIC REMISSION (CR) following induction chemotherapy for Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML), consistent with ELN 2022 complete remission criteria:
  - Myeloblasts less than 3% by bone marrow aspirate differential (2.5%)
  - No Auer rods identified
  - No blast clusters on core biopsy
  - Trilineage hematopoiesis with marrow regeneration
  - No circulating blasts in peripheral blood
  - ANC > 1,000/uL and platelets > 100,000/uL (hematologic recovery criteria met)

MINIMAL RESIDUAL DISEASE (MRD) STATUS: MRD-NEGATIVE
  - Multi-parameter flow cytometry MRD (sensitivity 0.01%): NEGATIVE
  - FLT3-ITD PCR (sensitivity 10^-4): NOT DETECTED
  - WT1 quantitative PCR: 26 copies/10^4 ABL1 (within normal range)

Summary: This post-induction day +35 bone marrow assessment demonstrates a complete morphologic and molecular remission (CRm/MRD-negative CR) following 7+3 induction chemotherapy with midostaurin in a patient with FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio AML with trisomy 8. Hypocellular marrow with trilineage regeneration is consistent with anticipated post-induction aplasia/recovery morphology. All available MRD modalities are negative at this time point.

Pathologist: [Hematopathology Attending]
Department of Hematopathology
Reviewed and signed out electronically.
